qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via malicious plugin installation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding achieves code execution through the plugin installer, it is not OS Command Injection (CWE-78). The vulnerability is in trusting attacker-controlled plugin manifest content that specifies system operations (sudoers rules, package installation, user creation). This is a privilege escalation vulnerability through inadequate plugin validation, not improper neutralization of special elements in OS commands where user input is injected into command strings.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Privilege escalation via OpenVPN configuration symlink attack
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is a path traversal vulnerability (CWE-22/23) leading to arbitrary symlink creation with sudo privileges, not OS Command Injection (CWE-78). The vulnerability uses escapeshellcmd() which allows path traversal sequences to pass through, enabling writes to arbitrary system directories. While this achieves privilege escalation, it is not command injection where user input is concatenated into OS commands.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described OS Command Injection vulnerability (CWE-78). The advisory describes a vulnerability where authenticated users can execute arbitrary OS commands through improper neutralization of special elements. Finding vuln_plugin_installer_rce identifies privilege escalation through trusted plugin manifest processing (not command injection). Finding vuln_openvpn_symlink_privilege_escalation identifies path traversal leading to arbitrary symlink creation (not command injection). Neither finding demonstrates the advisory root cause: user-controlled input being improperly escaped before use in OS command execution.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-24788 is classified as OS Command Injection (CWE-78) in RaspAP versions prior to 3.3.6
CWE-78 is defined as 'Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command'
vuln_plugin_installer_rce describes trust boundary violation in plugin manifest processing, not command string construction vulnerability
vuln_openvpn_symlink_privilege_escalation describes path traversal in file path construction (CWE-22/23), not command injection
Neither finding identifies a vulnerable endpoint where user input is concatenated into shell commands without proper escaping
The findings represent different vulnerability classes than the advisory-described OS command injection
